title: Letters from an American Farmer
author: J. Hector St. John de Crèvecœur
content: “The American is a new man, who acts upon new principles; he must therefore entertain new ideas, and form new opinions.”
action: Replace an outdated principle with a new strategy.

title: The Waste Land
author: T.S. Eliot
content: “These fragments I have shored against my ruins.”
action: Consolidate your resources to fortify one primary objective.

title: A Farewell to Arms
author: Ernest Hemingway
content: “The world breaks everyone and afterward many are strong at the broken places.”
action: Use your latest failure to build a more resilient system.

title: Chicago
author: Carl Sandburg
content: “Bragging and laughing that under his wrist is the pulse, and under his ribs the heart of the people.”
action: Identify the primary driver of your current project.

title: Notes of a Native Son
author: James Baldwin
content: “I imagine one of the reasons people cling to their hates so stubbornly is because they sense, once hate is gone, they will be forced to deal with pain.”
action: Resolve a persistent frustration instead of masking it.
